tools.name: postgres_service-create
tools.description: **This endpoint is in beta.** API contract is stable, and no breaking changes are expected in the future. Creates a new Postgres service in the organization and returns it. The service is started asynchronously. Required: organizationId, name, provider, region, size. Optional: haType, pgBouncerConfig, pgConfig (plus 2 more).

params.name: organizationId
params.description: ID of the organization that will own the service.

params.name: haType
params.description: Type of high availability: “none” for no replication, “async” for asynchronous replication to a single standby, and “sync” for synchronous replication to two standbys.

params.name: name
params.description: Name of the Postgres service. Alphanumerical string with whitespaces up to 50 characters.

params.name: pgBouncerConfig
params.description: PgBouncer [runtime configuration](https://www.pgbouncer.org/config.html) configuration.

params.name: pg-config-default-transaction-isolation
params.description: Sets the default transaction isolation level for new transactions.

params.name: pg-config-effective-cache-size
params.description: Sets the planner's assumption about the total size of data caches.

params.name: pg-config-effective-io-concurrency
params.description: Number of concurrent disk I/O operations the planner expects. Higher values (100-200) benefit SSDs.

params.name: pg-config-idle-in-transaction-session-timeout
params.description: Terminate any session that has been idle within an open transaction for longer than the specified time. Use 0 to disable.

params.name: pg-config-idle-session-timeout
params.description: Terminate any session that has been idle for longer than the specified time. Use 0 to disable.

params.name: pg-config-lock-timeout
params.description: Abort any statement that waits longer than the specified time while attempting to acquire a lock. Use 0 to disable.

params.name: pg-config-maintenance-work-mem
params.description: Sets the maximum memory to be used for maintenance operations.

params.name: pg-config-max-connections
params.description: Sets the maximum number of concurrent connections to the database server.

params.name: pg-config-max-parallel-maintenance-workers
params.description: Maximum number of parallel workers for maintenance operations like CREATE INDEX and VACUUM.

params.name: pg-config-max-parallel-workers
params.description: Maximum number of workers that can be used for parallel operations. Cannot exceed max_worker_processes.

params.name: pg-config-max-parallel-workers-per-gather
params.description: Maximum number of parallel workers per executor node for parallel queries. Use 0 to disable parallel queries.

params.name: pg-config-max-slot-wal-keep-size
params.description: Specifies the maximum size of WAL files that replication slots are allowed to retain. Use -1 for unlimited.

params.name: pg-config-max-wal-size
params.description: Maximum size WAL can grow between checkpoints. Larger values improve write performance but increase crash recovery time.

params.name: pg-config-max-worker-processes
params.description: Maximum number of background processes the system can support. Includes parallel query workers, logical replication, and more.

params.name: pg-config-min-wal-size
params.description: Minimum size to shrink the WAL to. WAL files are recycled rather than removed when below this size.

params.name: pg-config-random-page-cost
params.description: Sets the planner's estimate of the cost of a non-sequentially-fetched disk page. Lower values (1.1-1.5) are better for SSDs.

params.name: pg-config-ssl-min-protocol-version
params.description: Sets the minimum SSL/TLS protocol version allowed for client connections.

params.name: pg-config-statement-timeout
params.description: Abort any statement that runs longer than the specified time. Use 0 to disable.

params.name: pg-config-transaction-timeout
params.description: Terminate any statement that takes more than the specified time, even while active. Use 0 to disable.

params.name: pg-config-wal-compression
params.description: Compress full-page writes in WAL. Reduces I/O at the cost of CPU. Options vary by PostgreSQL version.

params.name: pg-config-wal-keep-size
params.description: Minimum size of past WAL files kept in pg_wal for standby servers. Use 0 to disable.

params.name: pg-config-wal-sender-timeout
params.description: Terminate replication connections that are inactive for longer than this time. Use 0 to disable.

params.name: pg-config-work-mem
params.description: Sets the amount of memory Postgres will use for internal operations like sorting and hashing as part of executing a query.

params.name: postgresVersion
params.description: Postgres version

params.name: provider
params.description: The cloud provider for a Postgres service.

params.name: region
params.description: The cloud region for a Postgres service.

params.name: size
params.description: The VM size for a Postgres service.

params.name: tags
params.description: Tags associated with the Postgres service.

tools.name: services_replica-scaling_instance-update
tools.description: Updates minimum and maximum memory limits per replica and idle mode scaling behavior for the service. Supports both vertical autoscaling (fixed replica count, variable memory) and horizontal autoscaling (variable replica count, fixed memory). The memory settings are available only for 'production' services and must be a multiple of 4 starting from 8GB. For vertical autoscaling, please contact support to enable adjustment of numReplicas. For horizontal autoscaling (minReplicas/maxReplicas/replicaMemoryGb), contact support to enable the feature for your organization. Required: organizationId, serviceId. Optional: idleScaling, idleTimeoutMinutes, maxReplicaMemoryGb (plus 5 more). Returns the updated ReplicaScalingInstanceUpdateResponse.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: ID of the organization that owns the service.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: ID of the service to update scaling parameters.

params.name: idleScaling
params.description: When set to true the service is allowed to scale down to zero when idle. True by default.

params.name: idleTimeoutMinutes
params.description: Set minimum idling timeout (in minutes). Must be >= 5 minutes.

params.name: maxReplicaMemoryGb
params.description: Maximum auto-scaling memory in Gb for a single replica. Available only for 'production' services. Must be a multiple of 4 and lower than or equal to 120 for non paid services or 356 for paid services. Used for vertical autoscaling. Mutually exclusive with replicaMemoryGb.

params.name: maxReplicas
params.description: Maximum number of replicas for horizontal autoscaling. Must be provided together with minReplicas. Mutually exclusive with numReplicas. Requires horizontal autoscaling to be enabled for the service.

params.name: minReplicaMemoryGb
params.description: Minimum auto-scaling memory in Gb for a single replica. Available only for 'production' services. Must be a multiple of 4 and greater than or equal to 8. Used for vertical autoscaling. Mutually exclusive with replicaMemoryGb.

params.name: minReplicas
params.description: Minimum number of replicas for horizontal autoscaling. Must be provided together with maxReplicas. Mutually exclusive with numReplicas. Requires horizontal autoscaling to be enabled for the service.

params.name: numReplicas
params.description: Fixed replica count for vertical autoscaling. Mutually exclusive with minReplicas/maxReplicas. Please contact support to enable adjustment of numReplicas. When switching from horizontal autoscaling mode the existing replica memory is preserved as the new vertical min/max range; include minReplicaMemoryGb/maxReplicaMemoryGb in the same request to set an explicit range.

params.name: replicaMemoryGb
params.description: Fixed memory per replica in Gb for horizontal autoscaling. Must be a multiple of 4, at least 8 Gb, and at most 120 Gb for non paid services or 356 Gb for paid services. Mutually exclusive with minReplicaMemoryGb/maxReplicaMemoryGb. Requires horizontal autoscaling to be enabled for the service.

tools.name: services_scaling_instance-update
tools.description: Updates minimum and maximum total memory limits and idle mode scaling behavior for the service. The memory settings are available only for 'production' services and must be a multiple of 12 starting from 24GB. Please contact support to enable adjustment of numReplicas. Required: organizationId, serviceId. Optional: idleScaling, idleTimeoutMinutes, maxTotalMemoryGb (plus 2 more). Returns the updated ScalingInstanceUpdateResponse.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: ID of the organization that owns the service.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: ID of the service to update scaling parameters.

params.name: idleScaling
params.description: When set to true the service is allowed to scale down to zero when idle. True by default.

params.name: idleTimeoutMinutes
params.description: Set minimum idling timeout (in minutes). Must be >= 5 minutes.

params.name: maxTotalMemoryGb
params.description: DEPRECATED - inaccurate for services with non-default numbers of replicas. Use `maxReplicaMemoryGb` instead. Maximum memory of three workers during auto-scaling in Gb. Available only for 'production' services. Must be a multiple of 12 and lower than or equal to 360 for non paid services or 1068 for paid services. Always absent for horizontal-autoscaling services (replica count is variable).

params.name: minTotalMemoryGb
params.description: DEPRECATED - inaccurate for services with non-default numbers of replicas. Use `minReplicaMemoryGb` instead. Minimum memory of three workers during auto-scaling in Gb. Available only for 'production' services. Must be a multiple of 12 and greater than or equal to 24. Always absent for horizontal-autoscaling services (replica count is variable).

params.name: numReplicas
params.description: Number of replicas for the service. The number of replicas must be between 2 and 20 for the first service in a warehouse. Services that are created in an existing warehouse can have a number of replicas as low as 1. Further restrictions may apply based on your organization's tier. It defaults to 1 for the BASIC tier and 3 for the SCALE and ENTERPRISE tiers.

tools.name: services_scaling-schedule_delete
tools.description: **Disclaimer:** This beta endpoint is evolving; the API contract may change. Deletes the autoscaling schedule for a service. If a schedule entry is currently active, the base scaling config is restored to the instance before the schedule is removed. Returns 404 if no schedule exists. Requires the scheduled autoscaling feature to be enabled for the organization. Required: organizationId, serviceId. Destructive.

tools.name: services_scaling-schedule_get
tools.description: **Disclaimer:** This beta endpoint is evolving; the API contract may change. Returns the autoscaling schedule for a service. Returns 404 if no schedule has been configured or if the schedule was cleared. Requires the scheduled autoscaling feature to be enabled for the organization. Required: organizationId, serviceId.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: ID of the organization that owns the service.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: ID of the service.

tools.name: services_scaling-schedule_upsert
tools.description: **Disclaimer:** This beta endpoint is evolving; the API contract may change. Creates or fully replaces the autoscaling schedule for a service. Pass an empty `entries` array to clear the schedule — a subsequent GET will return 404, and the response will contain an empty `baseConfig` (all fields absent). The base scaling config (applied when no entry is active) is managed separately via the `replicaScaling` endpoint. Requires the scheduled autoscaling feature to be enabled for the organization. Required: organizationId, serviceId, entries. Returns the new ScalingScheduleUpsertResponse.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: ID of the organization that owns the service.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: ID of the service.

params.name: entries
params.description: List of schedule entries. Pass an empty array to clear the schedule.

tools.name: services_upgrade-window_delete
tools.description: Deletes the upgrade window for a service, restoring the default scheduling behaviour. The upgrade window can only be deleted on primary services. Deletion succeeds even if the organization has lost the scheduled upgrades entitlement, so a window can be cleared after entitlement loss. Errors: - 400: the service is a secondary service. - 401: missing, invalid, or disabled API key. - 403: caller lacks `control-plane:service:manage` on the service. - 404: service does not exist, is not visible to the caller, or no upgrade window is configured. Required: organizationId, serviceId. Returns the UpgradeWindowDeleteResponse. Destructive.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: ID of the organization that owns the service.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: ID of the service.

tools.name: services_upgrade-window_get
tools.description: Returns the configured upgrade window for a service. Errors: - 401: missing, invalid, or disabled API key. - 403: caller lacks `control-plane:service:view` on the service. - 404: service does not exist, is not visible to the caller, or no upgrade window has been configured. Required: organizationId, serviceId.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: ID of the organization that owns the service.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: ID of the service.

tools.name: services_upgrade-window_update
tools.description: Creates or fully replaces the upgrade window for a service. The upgrade window currently lasts 6 hours from `startHourUtc`. The upgrade window can only be set on primary services; secondary services inherit the primary service window. Errors: - 400: invalid field values (`weekday` not in 0–6, `startHourUtc` not in {0, 6, 12, 18}), or the service is a secondary service. - 401: missing, invalid, or disabled API key. - 403: caller lacks `control-plane:service:manage` on the service, or the organization does not have the scheduled upgrades feature enabled. - 404: service does not exist or is not visible to the caller. Required: organizationId, serviceId, startHourUtc, weekday. Returns the updated UpgradeWindowUpdateResponse.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: ID of the organization that owns the service.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: ID of the service.

params.name: startHourUtc
params.description: UTC hour when the upgrade window starts. Must be one of 0, 6, 12, or 18. The upgrade window currently lasts 6 hours from this start time.

params.name: weekday
params.description: Day of the week the upgrade window starts. 0 = Sunday, 1 = Monday, …, 6 = Saturday.

tools.name: usage-cost_get
tools.description: Returns a grand total and a list of daily, per-entity organization usage cost records for the organization in the queried time period (maximum 31 days). All days in both the request and the response are evaluated based on the UTC timezone. Required: organizationId, from_date, to_date. Optional: filter.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: ID of the requested organization.

params.name: from_date
params.description: Start date for the report, e.g. 2024-12-19.

params.name: to_date
params.description: End date (inclusive) for the report, e.g. 2024-12-20. This date cannot be more than 30 days after from_date (for a maximum queried period of 31 days).

params.name: filter
params.description: Filter criteria to apply when retrieving the usage cost report. Currently, only filtering by resource tags is supported.
